records.question: What are the 5 best Accounting software for an Australian small business?

recommended_top_5.name: Xero
recommended_top_5.reason: Best all-round choice for most Australian small businesses: strong cloud accounting, bank reconciliation, reporting, STP-compliant payroll, super/PAYG support, and a large app/adviser ecosystem. ([xero.com](https://www.xero.com/au/accounting-software/?msockid=0e643c11bdde6b0700632af1bca56a53&utm_source=openai))

recommended_top_5.name: MYOB Business
recommended_top_5.reason: Excellent for Australian compliance and payroll-heavy small businesses, with built-in GST, BAS, Single Touch Payroll, superannuation and employee self-service features. ([myob.com](https://www.myob.com/au?utm_source=openai))

recommended_top_5.name: QuickBooks Online Australia
recommended_top_5.reason: Strong cloud accounting platform with automated bank feeds, GST tracking, BAS e-lodgement, invoicing, expenses and payroll integrations; also recognised by Canstar Blue for customer satisfaction. ([quickbooks.intuit.com](https://quickbooks.intuit.com/au/?utm_source=openai))

recommended_top_5.name: Reckon One
recommended_top_5.reason: Good value Australian-focused option for cost-conscious small businesses, offering invoicing, bank feeds, GST/BAS tracking, STP payroll, Payday Super readiness and unlimited users on accounting plans. ([reckon.com](https://www.reckon.com/au/?utm_source=openai))

recommended_top_5.name: Zoho Books
recommended_top_5.reason: Best budget-friendly alternative, especially for sole traders and service businesses already using Zoho; includes invoicing, bank feeds, reconciliation, GST/BAS support, automation and a free plan. ([zoho.com](https://www.zoho.com/au/books/small-business-accounting-software/?utm_source=openai))
